title: Determine if a benefit is taxable - Canada.ca
content:
## Determine if a benefit is taxable
- Select the link in the following table to determine if the specific benefit or allowance you provided is taxable . If the benefit or allowance is taxable, you have to include the value in your employee's income. Overview: What is a taxable benefit
Select the link in the following table to determine if the specific benefit or allowance you provided is taxable . If the benefit or allowance is taxable, you have to include the value in your employee's income.
- If the benefit or allowance is taxable , the following table summarizes if you must withhold CPP or EI on the value of the benefit, which codes to use to report the benefit or allowance and if GST/HST must be included in the value of the benefit. Special situation for CPP: Only remuneration is a non-cash taxable benefit
If the benefit or allowance is taxable , the following table summarizes if you must withhold CPP or EI on the value of the benefit, which codes to use to report the benefit or allowance and if GST/HST must be included in the value of the benefit.
Benefits and allowances
Automobile or motor vehicle benefits – Allowances or reimbursements provided to an employee for the use of their own vehicle
Automobile provided by the employer
Benefits provided to an employee who has a severe or prolonged impairment (disability)

Do not deduct CPP contributions if all of the following apply:
- It is a non-cash taxable benefit (with the exception of security options)
- It is the only remuneration you provide to your employee (your employee did not receive any cash earnings)
You must withhold EI if cash earnings were also paid in the pay period.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
